title: Troubleshooting
description: The mistakes that bite most often when you are new to CReact, what causes each, and how to fix it.
key_symptom: A component throws at render because a resource has no key.
key_cause: Every JSX element backed by <0>useAsyncOutput</0> needs a unique <1>key</1>. The key is the resource's identity — how the runtime matches it against persisted state across runs.
key_fix: Add a stable, unique <0>key</0> to the element. For lists, derive it from the data with <1>keyFn</1>.
one_output_symptom: A second <0>useAsyncOutput</0> in the same component behaves unpredictably.
one_output_cause: A component owns exactly one resource. Calling the hook twice gives them the same identity.
one_output_fix: Move the extra resource into a child component with its own key. Composition, not multiple hooks, is how you add resources.
undefined_symptom: Reading <0>db.url()</0> returns <1>undefined</1> on the first tick.
undefined_cause: Output accessors are empty until the handler's first <0>setOutputs</0> lands. Effects run before the async handler resolves.
undefined_fix: Read with a fallback — <0>db.url() ?? ""</0> — or gate the dependent work behind <1>Show</1> until the value exists.
idempotent_symptom: Two intervals fire, or a subscription is registered twice, after the process restarts.
idempotent_cause: Handlers re-run on every startup to re-establish runtime side effects. A handler that appends instead of reconciling doubles up.
idempotent_fix: Write handlers to be idempotent: restore state with <0>setOutputs(prev => …)</0> and set up side effects fresh, returning a cleanup that tears them down.
store_order_symptom: A store resets to its initial value on restart instead of restoring.
store_order_cause: The runtime attaches a store to the fiber only if it is created before the component's resource.
store_order_fix: Call <0>createStore</0> before <1>useAsyncOutput</1> in the component body.
heading_async_effect: An effect does not re-run when expected
async_effect_symptom: An effect ignores a signal it reads after an <0>await</0>.
async_effect_cause: Only synchronous reads subscribe. Anything read after an await is outside the tracking scope.
async_effect_fix: Read every signal synchronously at the top of the effect, then do async work with the captured values.
heading_props: A handler does not re-run when a prop changes
props_symptom: Changing a prop leaves the resource on its old inputs.
props_cause: Static props are read once. The handler re-runs on change only when its props are reactive.
props_fix: Pass props as a getter — <0>() => ({ bucket: name() })</0> — so the runtime re-runs the handler when a dependency changes.
tsconfig_symptom: The compiler errors on JSX, or elements render as the wrong type.
tsconfig_cause: The JSX transform is not pointed at CReact.
more_intro: Re-read the model and the reference:
more_playground: Try a change live in an example
